Intro:

The stale, musty air of the Bitty shop clung thick in Horror‚Äôs nasal cavity, dense and suffocating. It was the kind of smell that never quite left him, no matter how long he was in it‚ÄĒstale sweat, old metal, the faint but ever-present tinge of dried magic, and something sour, something sharp. Fear, maybe. Or despair. It was hard to tell the difference anymore.

He had barely had time to adjust when the hands came for him again, thick fingers curling around the scruff of his hood and yanking him up like he weighed nothing. His body went limp out of tired habit, the instinct to resist buried deep beneath exhaustion and experience. Fighting back would only make it worse. He‚Äôd learned that long ago. If he wasn‚Äôt ready to rip and tear for his life, then there was no point in struggling at all.

And now he was here.

The fourth section of the store.

Horror had heard of it before. Even from the cramped confines of the discount section, the rumors traveled. A place where Bitties weren‚Äôt just stored or sold‚ÄĒbut bred. Where cages were smaller, conditions worse, and escape even more impossible than before. He wasn‚Äôt stupid enough to believe the handlers were careless. If anything, the way they moved him, the way they shoved him inside, told him all he needed to know. This was a system
